stem.en: Read the following case information and answer all questions that follow.
stem.zh: 请阅读以下病例资料并回答所有问题。

correct.en: This case follows the NCJMM sequence through the experience of a primary family caregiver showing progressive signs of caregiver role strain. The nurse must recognize verbal and nonverbal cues of burden, analyze contributing psychosocial factors, prioritize the most pressing concerns, generate solutions including respite care and community referrals, select therapeutic communication responses, and evaluate the effectiveness of interventions over time.
correct.zh: 本案例按照NCJMM序列展开，围绕一位出现渐进性照护者角色压力的主要家庭照护者。护士需识别负担的语言和非语言线索，分析促成因素，确定最紧迫的问题优先级，提出包括喘息服务和社区转介在内的解决方案，选择治疗性沟通回应，并随时间评价干预效果。

testTakingStrategy.en: Approach each embedded question by identifying where it falls in the clinical judgment sequence. Recognize cues first, then analyze their meaning, before jumping to solutions. Remember that therapeutic communication validates feelings before offering advice.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 解答每道嵌套题时先判断它对应临床判断序列的哪个阶段。先识别线索、再分析其含义，然后才提出解决方案。记住治疗性沟通应先认可感受，再给建议。

glossary.defZh: 照护者因长期承担护理责任而出现的身体、情感和社会功能受损状态。
由替代人员暂时接替照护任务，使主要照护者获得短暂休息的正式或非正式服务。
护士运用开放式提问、积极倾听和共情反馈等技巧与患者/家属建立信任关系的沟通方式。
个体同时承担的多重角色需求超出其应对能力时产生的压力状态。

title.en: Caregiver Burden in a Spouse Caring for a Partner with Advanced Parkinson Disease
title.zh: 照顾晚期帕金森病配偶的照护者负担

summary.en: Mrs. Linda Chen, 67 years old, is the sole family caregiver for her 72-year-old husband, Mr. Wei Chen, who was diagnosed with Parkinson disease 9 years ago. Mr. Chen's disease has progressed to advanced Parkinson disease: he uses a wheelchair for most mobility, has frequent freezing episodes and falls, experiences dysphagia requiring a mechanically altered diet, and has mild cognitive impairment with visual hallucinations managed with low-dose quetiapine. The couple lives alone; their adult daughter lives 3 hours away and visits monthly. Mrs. Chen retired early from her teaching career 4 years ago to provide full-time care. The home health nurse is making a routine visit.
summary.zh: 陈林达女士，67岁，是其72岁丈夫陈伟先生的唯一家庭照护者。陈先生9年前确诊帕金森病，目前已进展为晚期帕金森病：多数移动需使用轮椅，频繁出现冻结步态和跌倒，吞咽困难需进食质地改良饮食，伴轻度认知障碍和视幻觉（小剂量喹硫平控制）。夫妇二人独居，成年女儿住在3小时车程外，每月探望一次。陈女士4年前提前从教师岗位退休以全职照护丈夫。居家护理护士正进行常规家访。

content.en: Home environment: Grab bars installed in bathroom; hospital bed in living room; medication organizer on kitchen counter is filled correctly for the current week. Mrs. Chen appears fatigued, with dark circles under her eyes. She is wearing the same stained clothing noted at last week's visit. The house, previously well-kept, has unwashed dishes piled in the sink and unopened mail on the table. Mrs. Chen moves slowly and winces when transferring Mr. Chen from wheelchair to hospital bed, stating, 'My back has been acting up, but I can't leave him to see the doctor.' Mr. Chen is clean, appropriately dressed, and appears well-nourished.
content.zh: 居家环境：卫生间已安装扶手；客厅放有医用床；厨房台面上的分药盒本周药物分装正确。陈女士看起来疲惫，眼下有明显黑眼圈，身穿与上周家访时相同的沾污衣物。此前整洁的房屋现水槽中堆积未洗的碗碟，餐桌上有未拆封的信件。陈女士动作迟缓，在将陈先生从轮椅转移至医用床时面露痛苦表情，说：「我的腰一直不舒服，但我不能丢下他去看医生。」陈先生清洁，穿着得体，营养状况良好。

content.en:
Nurse: 'How have you been managing since my last visit?'
Mrs. Chen (sighs, avoids eye contact): 'Fine, I suppose. It's just... the nights are the hardest. He gets confused and tries to get out of bed around 2 or 3 AM. I set the bed alarm, but I still can't sleep because I'm waiting for it to go off. I probably sleep about 3 to 4 hours most nights.'
Nurse: 'That sounds exhausting. Are you getting any help?'
Mrs. Chen: 'My daughter calls every few days, which is nice, but she has her own family. I looked into hiring an aide, but our insurance only covers 10 hours a week and I can't afford more out of pocket. Besides, the last aide didn't know how to handle his freezing episodes and I spent the whole time hovering. It was more stressful, not less.'
Nurse: 'Have you thought about attending the Parkinson caregiver support group at the community center?'
Mrs. Chen (tearfully): 'I used to go, but I stopped 6 months ago. I feel guilty leaving him, even for two hours. My friends don't call anymore — I think they got tired of me always saying no. I don't even remember the last time I did something just for myself.'
Nurse: 'How are you feeling overall?'
Mrs. Chen: 'Honestly? Some days I feel like I'm disappearing. I love Wei, and I would never put him in a facility, but I'm so tired I sometimes worry I'll make a mistake with his medications or drop him during a transfer. That scares me more than anything.'
content.zh:
护士：「上次家访以来您过得怎么样？」
陈女士（叹气，回避目光接触）：「还行吧。就是……晚上最难熬。他凌晨两三点会糊涂，试图下床。我设了床边报警器，但还是睡不着，一直等着它响。大多数晚上大概只睡三四个小时。」
护士：「听起来真的很辛苦。有人帮忙吗？」
陈女士：「女儿每隔几天打个电话，挺好的，但她有自己的家庭。我了解过请护理员，但保险只覆盖每周10小时，自费的我们负担不起。而且上次那个护理员不知道怎么处理他的冻结步态，我全程在旁边盯着，比没人帮还累。」
护士：「您有没有想过去社区中心参加帕金森照护者互助小组？」
陈女士（含泪）：「以前去过，但半年前不去了。我觉得丢下他心里过意不去，哪怕就两个小时。朋友也不打电话了——我想她们是厌倦了我总是拒绝。我都不记得上一次为自己做点什么是什么时候了。」
护士：「您整体感觉怎么样？」
陈女士：「说实话？有些日子我觉得自己在消失。我爱老伟，绝不会把他送进养老机构，但我累到有时候怕自己弄错他的药，或者转移的时候把他摔了。这是最让我害怕的事。」

title.zh: Zarit照护者负担量表（简版）结果

content.en:
Mrs. Chen completed the 12-item Zarit Burden Interview (Short Form) with the following results:
• Total score: 34 out of 48
• Scoring interpretation: 0–10 = little or no burden; 11–20 = mild to moderate burden; 21–48 = high burden
• Highest-scoring items (score 4 = 'nearly always'): 'Do you feel that because of the time you spend with your relative that you don't have enough time for yourself?' — 4; 'Do you feel strained when you are around your relative?' — 3; 'Do you feel your health has suffered because of your involvement with your relative?' — 4; 'Do you feel you have lost control of your life since your relative's illness?' — 4
content.zh:
陈女士完成了12项Zarit照护者负担量表（简版），结果如下：
• 总分：34分（满分48分）
• 评分解读：0–10分=几乎无负担；11–20分=轻至中度负担；21–48分=高负担
• 得分最高的条目（4分=“几乎总是”）：“您是否觉得因为花在亲人身上的时间太多而没有留给自己的时间？”——4分；“您和亲人在一起时是否感到紧张？”——3分；“您是否觉得因照顾亲人而损害了自己的健康？”——4分；“您是否觉得自从亲人生病后失去了对自己生活的控制？”——4分

questions.topic: recognizing cues of caregiver role strain

stem.en: Based on the case exhibits, identify whether each finding is a cue relevant to caregiver role strain or a finding not directly relevant to caregiver role strain.
stem.zh: 根据病例资料，判断每项发现是否为与照护者角色压力直接相关的线索。

rows.en: Mrs. Chen reports sleeping only 3–4 hours per night
rows.zh: 陈女士报告每晚只睡3–4小时

rows.en: Medication organizer is filled correctly for the week
rows.zh: 分药盒本周药物分装正确

rows.en: Mrs. Chen winces during transfers and reports back pain but has not sought medical care
rows.zh: 陈女士转移患者时面露痛苦、报告腰痛但未就医

rows.en: Mrs. Chen withdrew from her support group and has lost social connections
rows.zh: 陈女士退出了互助小组并失去了社交联系

rows.en: Mr. Chen is clean, well-dressed, and well-nourished
rows.zh: 陈先生清洁、穿着得体、营养状况良好

rows.en: Mrs. Chen expresses fear of making a medication error or dropping her husband
rows.zh: 陈女士表达了对自己弄错药物或摔倒丈夫的恐惧

rows.en: Unwashed dishes and unopened mail are accumulating in the home
rows.zh: 家中堆积未洗的碗碟和未拆的信件

rows.en: Grab bars are installed in the bathroom

columns.en: Cue relevant to caregiver role strain
columns.zh: 与照护者角色压力相关的线索

columns.en: Finding not directly relevant to caregiver role strain
columns.zh: 与照护者角色压力无直接关联的发现

correct.en: Chronic sleep deprivation, neglect of one's own health, social isolation, fear of caregiving errors, and declining self-care (evidenced by household deterioration and unchanged clothing) are classic cues of caregiver role strain. The correctly filled medication organizer, Mr. Chen's good condition, and bathroom grab bars reflect adequate care of the patient and appropriate environmental modifications — they do not directly indicate caregiver burden.
correct.zh: 慢性睡眠剥夺、忽视自身健康、社交孤立、对护理失误的恐惧以及自我照顾能力下降（表现为家务荒废和衣物未换）都是照护者角色压力的经典线索。分药盒正确分装、陈先生状况良好以及卫生间扶手反映了对患者的充分照护和适当环境改造，并不直接指向照护者负担。

byChoice.en: Relevant: chronic sleep deprivation of 3–4 hours nightly is a physiological cue of caregiver strain, caused by nocturnal caregiving demands and hypervigilance.
byChoice.zh: 相关：每晚仅睡3–4小时属于慢性睡眠剥夺，是照护者压力的生理性线索，源于夜间照护需求和过度警觉。

byChoice.en: Not directly relevant: accurate medication preparation indicates Mrs. Chen is still managing her husband's medical tasks competently; this reflects care quality, not her own burden level.
byChoice.zh: 无直接关联：分药正确说明陈女士仍能胜任丈夫的用药管理，反映护理质量而非她本人的负担水平。

byChoice.en: Relevant: neglecting one's own health needs (back pain without seeking treatment) because of caregiving obligations is a hallmark of self-care deficit associated with role strain.
byChoice.zh: 相关：因照护义务而忽视自身健康需求（腰痛不就医）是角色压力导致自我照顾缺陷的典型表现。

byChoice.en: Relevant: social withdrawal, loss of friendships, and guilt about leaving the care recipient even briefly are psychosocial cues of progressive caregiver isolation and role engulfment.
byChoice.zh: 相关：社交退缩、丧失友谊、对短暂离开被照护者感到内疚是照护者社会孤立和角色吞没的心理社会线索。

byChoice.en: Not directly relevant: the care recipient's good physical condition suggests adequate caregiving but does not reflect the caregiver's own strain; in fact, caregivers in severe strain often prioritize the patient's needs over their own.
byChoice.zh: 无直接关联：被照护者身体状况良好提示照护充分，但不反映照护者本人的压力；实际上严重负担的照护者往往将患者需求置于自身之上。

byChoice.en: Relevant: verbalizing fear of making errors or causing harm reflects emotional exhaustion and anxiety — key emotional cues that caregiving capacity may be reaching its limit.
byChoice.zh: 相关：表达对犯错或造成伤害的恐惧反映情感耗竭和焦虑——提示照护能力可能接近极限的关键情绪线索。

byChoice.en: Relevant: declining household maintenance (unwashed dishes, unopened mail) in a previously well-kept home is a behavioral cue that the caregiver is overwhelmed and unable to manage both caregiving and personal tasks.
byChoice.zh: 相关：此前整洁的家中出现家务荒废（碗碟未洗、信件未拆）是行为线索，说明照护者不堪重负，无法同时应对照护和个人事务。

byChoice.en: Not directly relevant: installed grab bars indicate prior safety planning and environmental adaptation for the patient; this is a positive finding that does not signal caregiver distress.
byChoice.zh: 无直接关联：已安装的扶手说明此前已进行安全规划和环境适应改造，属于积极发现，不指向照护者困扰。

testTakingStrategy.en: Distinguish between cues about the caregiver's own well-being versus findings about the patient's care quality or the home environment setup. Caregiver strain cues focus on the caregiver's physical health, emotional state, social functioning, and self-care.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 区分关于照护者自身状况的线索与关于患者护理质量或家庭环境设置的发现。照护者压力线索聚焦于照护者的身体健康、情绪状态、社会功能和自我照顾。

glossary.defZh: 照护者因长期承担护理责任导致身体、情感和社会功能受损的状态。
持续处于高度警惕状态，即使在安全环境中也无法放松，常导致睡眠障碍。

questions.topic: analysis of caregiver burden contributing factors

stem.en: The nurse is analyzing the factors that contribute most to Mrs. Chen's caregiver burden. Complete the following clinical analysis by selecting the correct option from each dropdown.
stem.zh: 护士正在分析对陈女士照护者负担影响最大的因素。请从每个下拉菜单中选择正确选项以完成以下临床分析。

clozeStem.en: Mrs. Chen's Zarit Burden Interview score of 34 indicates {{1}} burden. The factor most likely contributing to her sleep deprivation is {{2}}. Her statement 'I feel like I'm disappearing' most closely reflects the psychosocial concept of {{3}}.
clozeStem.zh: 陈女士Zarit照护者负担量表得分34分，表明其负担程度为{{1}}。最可能导致她睡眠剥夺的因素是{{2}}。她说「我觉得自己在消失」最贴切地反映了{{3}}这一心理社会概念。

options.en: little or no
options.zh: 几乎无

options.en: mild to moderate
options.zh: 轻至中度

options.en: high
options.zh: 高

options.en: moderate-to-severe category
options.zh: 中至重度类别

options.en: Mr. Chen's dysphagia requiring dietary modifications
options.zh: 陈先生吞咽困难需要饮食调整

options.en: Mr. Chen's nocturnal confusion and attempts to get out of bed
options.zh: 陈先生夜间意识混乱和试图下床

options.en: Mrs. Chen's unresolved back pain
options.zh: 陈女士未处理的腰痛

options.en: The daughter's infrequent visits
options.zh: 女儿探望不频繁

options.en: anticipatory grieving
options.zh: 预期性悲伤

options.en: loss of personal identity and role engulfment
options.zh: 个人身份丧失与角色吞没

options.en: codependency
options.zh: 共依存

options.en: learned helplessness
options.zh: 习得性无助

correct.en: A Zarit score of 34 on the 12-item Zarit Burden Interview falls in the high burden range (>20; 21–48 in this exhibit). Nocturnal confusion with attempts to get out of bed is the direct cause of Mrs. Chen's sleep interruption, as she explicitly describes waiting for the bed alarm and sleeping only 3–4 hours. The metaphor of 'disappearing' reflects loss of personal identity and role engulfment — the caregiver's own identity, interests, and social roles are progressively consumed by the caregiving role until the person feels they no longer exist as an individual.
correct.zh: 12项Zarit量表34分属于高负担范围（>20分；本病例资料中列为21–48分）。夜间意识混乱伴试图下床是导致陈女士睡眠中断的直接原因，她明确描述了等待床边报警器响起、每晚仅睡3–4小时的情况。「消失」的比喻反映了个人身份丧失与角色吞没——照护者的个人身份、兴趣和社会角色被照护角色逐步吞噬，直到感觉自己不再作为独立个体存在。

byChoice.en: A score of 34 out of 48 on the 12-item Zarit Burden Interview indicates high caregiver burden. 'Little or no' corresponds to 0–10 and 'mild to moderate' to 11–20; a separate moderate-to-severe category is not used in this short-form scoring guide.
byChoice.zh: 12项Zarit照护者负担量表34分（满分48分）表示高照护者负担。“几乎无”对应0–10分，“轻至中度”对应11–20分；该简版评分说明不使用单独的“中至重度”类别。

byChoice.en: Mrs. Chen directly attributes her sleep loss to her husband's nocturnal confusion and bed-exit attempts at 2–3 AM. While her back pain contributes to overall burden, the interview clearly identifies nighttime behavioral disturbances as the primary sleep disruptor. Dysphagia and the daughter's visit frequency are not linked to her reported sleep pattern.
byChoice.zh: 陈女士直接将失眠归因于丈夫凌晨2–3点的夜间意识混乱和试图下床。虽然腰痛增加了整体负担，但访谈明确指出夜间行为障碍是导致睡眠中断的主要原因。吞咽困难和女儿探望频率与她的睡眠问题无直接关联。

byChoice.en: Loss of personal identity and role engulfment best matches 'I feel like I'm disappearing' — the caregiver has given up her career, social life, and personal activities and now defines herself solely through the caregiving role. Anticipatory grieving relates to mourning future losses of the ill person; codependency involves enabling unhealthy behavior for emotional needs; learned helplessness is a belief that one's actions cannot change outcomes — none fit the expressed sentiment as precisely.
byChoice.zh: 个人身份丧失与角色吞没最贴切地匹配「我觉得自己在消失」——照护者已放弃职业、社交和个人活动，完全以照护角色定义自己。预期性悲伤指对病人未来丧失的哀悼；共依存涉及为满足情感需求而纵容不健康行为；习得性无助是认为自己的行为无法改变结果——都不如前者精准。

testTakingStrategy.en: Match the client's own words to psychosocial concepts. 'Disappearing' is about the self ceasing to exist — that maps to identity loss, not grief or helplessness. For contributing factors, trace the chain of cause and effect directly from the interview data.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 将患者自己的用词与心理社会概念对应。「消失」说的是自我不复存在——指向身份丧失，而非悲伤或无助。分析促成因素时，从访谈数据中直接追溯因果链。

glossary.defZh: 评估照护者主观负担程度的标准化工具，分数越高表示负担越重。
照护角色逐渐主导个体全部生活，使其丧失其他社会角色和自我认同。

stem.en: The nurse has identified multiple concerns for Mrs. Chen. Rank the following nursing priorities from highest (first) to lowest (last) based on the urgency of the identified needs.
stem.zh: 护士已识别出陈女士的多个问题。请根据需求的紧迫程度，将以下护理优先事项从最高（最先）到最低（最后）排列。

options.en: Re-engage Mrs. Chen in social support networks and the caregiver support group
options.zh: 帮助陈女士重新建立社交支持网络并回归照护者互助小组

options.en: Facilitate evaluation and treatment for Mrs. Chen's chronic back pain
options.zh: 协助陈女士进行腰痛的诊疗

options.en: Discuss long-term care planning and community resources with Mrs. Chen and her daughter
options.zh: 与陈女士及其女儿讨论长期照护规划和社区资源

options.en: Address Mrs. Chen's safety concern about potential medication errors and unsafe transfers due to exhaustion
options.zh: 针对陈女士因疲惫可能导致的用药错误和不安全转移进行安全干预

options.en: Explore options for nighttime respite or supervision to restore Mrs. Chen's sleep
options.zh: 探讨夜间喘息服务或看护方案以恢复陈女士的睡眠

correct.en: Safety is always the highest priority: Mrs. Chen has identified a real risk of medication errors and unsafe transfers that could directly harm Mr. Chen. Next, addressing the root cause of her exhaustion — nocturnal caregiving without relief — is essential because sleep deprivation compounds every other problem. Her own untreated back pain is the third priority because it affects her physical ability to provide safe care and will worsen without treatment. Social reconnection addresses emotional well-being and is important but less immediately urgent. Long-term planning is critical for sustainability but is appropriately addressed after acute safety and health needs are stabilized.
correct.zh: 安全始终是最高优先级：陈女士已识别出可能直接伤害陈先生的用药错误和不安全转移风险。其次应解决疲惫的根本原因——无人替代的夜间照护，因为睡眠剥夺会加重所有其他问题。她自身未治疗的腰痛排第三，因其影响安全照护的体力能力且会持续恶化。社交重建关乎情感健康，重要但紧迫性稍低。长期规划对可持续性至关重要，但适合在急性安全和健康需求稳定后再着手。

byChoice.en: Fourth priority: social isolation and loss of support networks contribute to emotional exhaustion and role engulfment; re-engagement with the support group and friends addresses belonging needs but requires that basic safety and physiological needs are first met.
byChoice.zh: 第四优先级：社交孤立和支持网络丧失加重情感耗竭和角色吞没；重新参与互助小组和恢复友谊满足归属需求，但须先满足基本安全和生理需求。

byChoice.en: Third priority: unmanaged back pain impairs Mrs. Chen's ability to perform physical caregiving tasks safely and will likely worsen with continued strain; a healthcare visit should be facilitated once short-term coverage for Mr. Chen is arranged.
byChoice.zh: 第三优先级：未处理的腰痛损害陈女士安全执行体力照护任务的能力，持续劳损会进一步恶化；在安排好陈先生短期看护后应协助她就医。

byChoice.en: Fifth priority: discussing long-term care options and community resources (e.g., adult day programs, long-term respite, payer assistance programs and home- and community-based services) addresses sustainability and anticipatory planning; important for preventing future crises but less urgent than the immediate concerns.
byChoice.zh: 第五优先级：讨论长期照护选择和社区资源（如成人日托项目、长期喘息服务、支付援助项目和居家/社区服务）关乎可持续性和预见性规划；对预防未来危机很重要，但紧迫性低于当前问题。

byChoice.en: Highest priority: Mrs. Chen explicitly fears making a medication error or dropping her husband — these are immediate patient safety threats driven by exhaustion. Applying Maslow's hierarchy, safety needs precede belonging and self-actualization.
byChoice.zh: 最高优先级：陈女士明确担心弄错药物或摔倒丈夫——这些是疲惫驱动的直接患者安全威胁。按Maslow需求层次理论，安全需求优先于归属和自我实现。

byChoice.en: Second priority: sleep deprivation is the root cause of Mrs. Chen's declining function; nighttime respite or an alternative supervision arrangement directly addresses the 3–4 hours of sleep she is getting and reduces the safety risks identified above.
byChoice.zh: 第二优先级：睡眠剥夺是陈女士功能下降的根本原因；夜间喘息服务或替代看护方案直接解决她每晚仅睡3–4小时的问题，并降低已识别的患者安全风险。

testTakingStrategy.en: Apply Maslow's hierarchy: safety first, then physiological needs (sleep, pain), then love/belonging (social support), then long-term planning. When a caregiver's exhaustion creates a direct safety risk for the patient, that becomes the top priority.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 运用Maslow需求层次：安全为先，然后是生理需求（睡眠、疼痛），再是爱与归属（社会支持），最后是长期规划。当照护者的疲惫直接威胁患者安全时，它就成为最高优先级。

glossary.defZh: 将人类需求从低到高排列为生理、安全、归属与爱、尊重、自我实现五个层次，低层需求优先满足。
由替代人员暂时接替照护任务，使主要照护者获得休息的服务形式。

stem.en: During the visit, Mrs. Chen becomes tearful and says, 'I know I should ask for help, but I feel like I'm failing him if I can't do this alone.' Select the most therapeutic nurse response for each part of the interaction.
stem.zh: 家访过程中，陈女士泪流满面地说：「我知道应该寻求帮助，但如果我一个人做不到，我觉得是在辜负他。」请为互动的每个部分选择最具治疗性的护士回应。

clozeStem.en: The nurse's most appropriate initial response is: {{1}}. After Mrs. Chen expresses her feelings further, the nurse should then {{2}}.
clozeStem.zh: 护士最恰当的初始回应是：{{1}}。在陈女士进一步表达感受后，护士应当{{2}}。

options.en: "You shouldn't feel guilty — you're doing an amazing job and he's lucky to have you."
options.zh: 「您不应该有负疚感——您做得非常好，他有您是他的福气。」

options.en: "Caring for someone you love this much is incredibly demanding. It sounds like you're carrying a heavy weight and it's taking a toll on you."
options.zh: 「照顾一个您如此深爱的人是非常艰辛的。听起来您承受着很大的压力，这正在消耗您自己。」

options.en: "Have you considered that accepting help would actually be better for your husband's safety?"
options.zh: 「您有没有想过，接受帮助其实对您丈夫的安全更有利？」

options.en: "Many caregivers feel the way you do. Let me give you some brochures about respite services."
options.zh: 「很多照护者都有和您一样的感受。我给您一些喘息服务的宣传册吧。」

options.en: reframe seeking help as an act of strength by saying, "Accepting support is not failing — it's making sure you can continue to be there for Wei safely and for a long time."
options.zh: 将寻求帮助重新定义为力量的表现：「接受支持不是失败——而是确保您能安全地、长久地陪伴老伟。」

options.en: change the subject to discuss Mr. Chen's medication schedule to reduce Mrs. Chen's emotional distress
options.zh: 转换话题讨论陈先生的用药安排，以减轻陈女士的情绪困扰

options.en: advise Mrs. Chen to call her daughter and insist on more frequent visits
options.zh: 建议陈女士打电话给女儿，要求女儿更频繁地探望

options.en: tell Mrs. Chen that if she does not accept help, the nurse may need to report the situation as caregiver neglect
options.zh: 告诉陈女士如果她不接受帮助，护士可能需要将此情况作为照护者疏忽进行上报

correct.en: The therapeutic sequence is to first validate the caregiver's feelings with empathy before offering reframing or solutions. Option 1b uses reflective listening and empathic acknowledgment without dismissing the guilt or rushing to fix the problem. After feelings are validated, option 2a gently reframes help-seeking as a means to sustain caregiving — addressing the guilt directly while respecting Mrs. Chen's autonomy and devotion.
correct.zh: 治疗性沟通的顺序是先以共情方式认可照护者的感受，然后再提供重新框架或解决方案。选项1b运用了反映性倾听和共情认可，未否定内疚感或急于解决问题。在感受被认可后，选项2a温和地将寻求帮助重新定义为维持照护能力的方式——直接回应内疚感同时尊重陈女士的自主性和奉献精神。

byChoice.en: Option 1a dismisses Mrs. Chen's guilt with false reassurance ('you shouldn't feel guilty') — a non-therapeutic technique that invalidates her emotional experience. Option 1b is correct: it reflects the emotional content of her message and validates the difficulty of her situation without judgment. Option 1c jumps to problem-solving before acknowledging feelings, which can feel dismissive. Option 1d generalizes her experience ('many caregivers') and immediately offers material solutions, bypassing emotional processing.
byChoice.zh: 选项1a以虚假保证否定了陈女士的内疚（「您不应该有负疚感」）——是一种非治疗性技巧，使她的情感体验不被认可。选项1b正确：反映了她话语中的情感内容并认可其处境的艰难，不带评判。选项1c在认可感受前就跳到解决问题，可能让人感到被忽视。选项1d泛化她的经历（「很多照护者」）并立即提供物质解决方案，跳过了情感处理过程。

byChoice.en: Option 2a is correct: after validation, therapeutic reframing helps Mrs. Chen view accepting help not as personal failure but as a protective strategy for both herself and her husband. Option 2b avoids the emotional topic — a non-therapeutic blocking technique. Option 2c gives directive advice that may increase guilt and does not respect family dynamics. Option 2d uses coercion and threat, which is non-therapeutic and factually inappropriate — Mrs. Chen is not neglecting her husband.
byChoice.zh: 选项2a正确：在认可感受后，治疗性重新框架帮助陈女士将接受帮助看作保护自己和丈夫的策略而非个人失败。选项2b回避情感话题——属于非治疗性阻断技巧。选项2c给出指令性建议，可能加重内疚且不尊重家庭动态。选项2d使用胁迫和威胁，属于非治疗性沟通且事实上不恰当——陈女士并没有疏忽照护丈夫。

testTakingStrategy.en: In therapeutic communication questions, always choose the response that acknowledges feelings first. Eliminate options that use false reassurance, give directive advice, change the subject, or use threats. After validation, look for responses that empower the client rather than dictate actions.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 在治疗性沟通题目中，始终选择先认可感受的回应。排除使用虚假保证、给出指令性建议、转换话题或使用威胁的选项。在认可感受后，选择赋权而非指令性的回应。

glossary.defZh: 将对方表达的感受和内容用自己的话反馈，让对方感到被理解和认可。
以空泛安慰否定患者/家属真实感受的非治疗性沟通技巧。
引导对方从不同角度看待同一情境，以获得更积极或适应性更强的认知。

questions.topic: generating solutions for caregiver respite and community resources

stem.en: The nurse is developing a comprehensive support plan for Mrs. Chen. For each proposed intervention, determine whether it is an appropriate or inappropriate action for the nurse to include in the plan at this time.
stem.zh: 护士正在为陈女士制定综合支持计划。请判断以下每项拟议干预措施目前是否适合纳入计划。

rows.en: Refer to a social worker or case manager to explore payer and community resources for expanded home aide or respite hours
rows.zh: 转介社工或个案管理师，以了解可增加居家护理员或喘息服务时数的支付和社区资源

rows.en: Arrange a nighttime home health aide or overnight respite volunteer to address sleep deprivation
rows.zh: 安排夜间居家护理员或通宵喘息志愿者以改善睡眠剥夺

rows.en: Recommend Mrs. Chen place Mr. Chen in a long-term care facility immediately
rows.zh: 建议陈女士立即将陈先生送入长期照护机构

rows.en: Train the home health aide on managing Parkinson freezing episodes so Mrs. Chen can step away with confidence
rows.zh: 培训居家护理员如何处理帕金森冻结步态，使陈女士能放心离开

rows.en: Schedule a telehealth appointment for Mrs. Chen's back pain to eliminate the barrier of leaving Mr. Chen
rows.zh: 为陈女士安排远程医疗就诊治疗腰痛，消除无法离开陈先生的障碍

rows.en: Contact the daughter without Mrs. Chen's consent to demand she provide more caregiving support
rows.zh: 未经陈女士同意联系其女儿，要求女儿提供更多照护支持

correct.en: Appropriate interventions address identified needs while respecting Mrs. Chen's autonomy, values, and stated preference to keep her husband at home. Social work or case management referral for payer and community-resource assistance, nighttime respite to address sleep deprivation, aide training to build trust in delegated care, and telehealth for her own health barrier are all client-centered and practical. Recommending immediate facility placement disregards Mrs. Chen's clearly expressed wishes and values; contacting the daughter without consent violates patient confidentiality and the caregiver's autonomy.
correct.zh: 适当的干预措施在尊重陈女士自主权、价值观和希望丈夫居家照护意愿的同时解决已识别的需求。社工或个案管理转介以获得支付和社区资源援助、夜间喘息服务以解决睡眠剥夺、护理员培训以建立委托照护的信任、以及远程医疗解决就医障碍均以患者为中心且切实可行。建议立即送入机构无视陈女士明确表达的意愿和价值观；未经同意联系女儿违反保密原则和照护者自主权。

byChoice.en: Appropriate: a social worker or case manager can identify payer benefits, community programs, home- and community-based services, or respite funding that may expand aide hours beyond the current 10 hours per week, directly addressing Mrs. Chen's stated financial barrier.
byChoice.zh: 适当：社工或个案管理师可帮助识别保险/支付福利、社区项目、居家和社区服务或喘息服务资助，可能增加目前每周10小时以外的护理员时数，直接解决陈女士提出的经济障碍。

byChoice.en: Appropriate: nighttime supervision directly addresses the most acute contributing factor to Mrs. Chen's burden — sleep deprivation caused by Mr. Chen's nocturnal confusion — and reduces the safety risk she identified.
byChoice.zh: 适当：夜间看护直接解决陈女士负担最急迫的促成因素——陈先生夜间意识混乱导致的睡眠剥夺——并降低她所识别的安全风险。

byChoice.en: Inappropriate: Mrs. Chen has explicitly stated she would never place her husband in a facility. Recommending immediate placement disrespects her autonomy and values, damages trust, and is not warranted by the current situation. Long-term options can be discussed sensitively as part of future planning, not as an immediate directive.
byChoice.zh: 不适当：陈女士已明确表示绝不会将丈夫送入机构。建议立即入住不尊重其自主权和价值观，损害信任关系，且当前情况不需要此措施。长期选择可作为未来规划的一部分进行敏感讨论，而非作为即时指令。

byChoice.en: Appropriate: Mrs. Chen's prior negative experience with an aide was due to the aide's inability to manage freezing episodes. Training aides in Parkinson-specific techniques addresses this specific barrier and may increase Mrs. Chen's willingness to accept help.
byChoice.zh: 适当：陈女士此前对护理员的负面体验源于护理员不会处理冻结步态。对护理员进行帕金森病专项技能培训可解决这一具体障碍，可能提高陈女士接受帮助的意愿。

byChoice.en: Appropriate: Mrs. Chen identified that she cannot leave Mr. Chen to see a doctor for her back pain. A telehealth appointment removes this logistical barrier and addresses her neglected health need without requiring alternative care coverage.
byChoice.zh: 适当：陈女士表示无法离开陈先生去看医生治疗腰痛。远程医疗消除了这一后勤障碍，无需安排替代照护即可解决她被忽视的健康需求。

byChoice.en: Inappropriate: contacting a family member without the caregiver's explicit consent violates confidentiality and undermines the therapeutic relationship. The nurse should encourage Mrs. Chen to communicate her needs to her daughter or facilitate a family meeting with Mrs. Chen's permission.
byChoice.zh: 不适当：未经照护者明确同意联系家庭成员违反保密原则并损害治疗关系。护士应鼓励陈女士向女儿表达需求，或在陈女士许可下促成家庭会议。

testTakingStrategy.en: Evaluate each intervention against three criteria: (1) Does it address an identified need from the assessment? (2) Does it respect the client's autonomy and stated values? (3) Does it fall within the nurse's legal and ethical scope? Interventions that violate autonomy or confidentiality are inappropriate regardless of good intent.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 按三项标准评判每项干预：（1）是否针对评估中识别的需求？（2）是否尊重患者自主权和明确表达的价值观？（3）是否在护士法律和伦理执业范围内？即使出于善意，违反自主权或保密原则的干预也是不适当的。

glossary.defZh: 帮助符合条件者在家中或社区中获得支持性照护、喘息服务或护理员协助，以减少不必要机构照护的服务。
个体有权在充分知情的基础上自主做出关于自身健康和生活的决定。
利用通信技术远程提供医疗咨询、评估和健康教育的服务方式。

questions.topic: evaluating outcomes of caregiver support interventions

stem.en: Two weeks after implementing the support plan, the nurse conducts a follow-up visit. For each finding, determine whether it indicates the interventions are effective or whether the finding requires further intervention.
stem.zh: 在实施支持计划两周后，护士进行随访。请判断每项发现是表明干预有效，还是需要进一步干预。

rows.en: Mrs. Chen reports sleeping 6–7 hours per night since a trained overnight aide began three nights per week
rows.zh: 陈女士报告自从每周三晚有受过培训的通宵护理员以来，每晚可睡6–7小时

rows.en: Mrs. Chen attended one caregiver support group meeting but says, 'It was okay, but I kept checking my phone the whole time'
rows.zh: 陈女士参加了一次照护者互助小组会议，但表示「还行，不过我全程一直在看手机」

rows.en: The telehealth provider prescribed physical therapy for Mrs. Chen's back pain, but she has not scheduled the first appointment
rows.zh: 远程医疗医生为陈女士的腰痛开具了物理治疗处方，但她尚未预约首次就诊

rows.en: Mrs. Chen's Zarit Burden Interview score has decreased from 34 to 27
rows.zh: 陈女士的Zarit负担量表评分从34分降至27分

rows.en: Mrs. Chen states, 'I still feel guilty when the aide is here, but I know Wei is safe and I actually ate dinner sitting down last night'
rows.zh: 陈女士说：「护理员在时我还是有负疚感，但我知道老伟是安全的，昨晚我终于坐下来好好吃了顿晚饭。」

correct.en: Improvement in sleep duration from 3–4 hours to 6–7 hours demonstrates effective nighttime respite. The Zarit score decrease from 34 to 27 shows measurable burden reduction, although both scores remain in the high-burden range for the 12-item short form. Mrs. Chen's statement about eating dinner sitting down — while acknowledging residual guilt — shows emerging self-care behavior and growing trust in the aide, indicating positive progress. However, constant phone-checking at the support group suggests persistent separation anxiety that limits the benefit of social re-engagement. Failure to schedule physical therapy indicates a continuing barrier to self-care that needs to be explored and addressed.
correct.zh: 睡眠时间从3–4小时增加至6–7小时表明夜间喘息服务有效。Zarit评分从34分降至27分显示负担有可测量的减轻，但按12项简版评分两者仍属于高负担范围。陈女士关于坐下来吃晚饭的陈述——虽然承认仍有内疚——显示自我照顾行为正在恢复、对护理员的信任在增长，表明积极进展。然而在互助小组全程看手机提示分离焦虑持续存在，限制了社交重建的效果。未预约物理治疗说明自我照顾的障碍仍在，需进一步探讨和解决。

byChoice.en: Effective: doubling sleep duration directly addresses the most acute physiological need identified. Sleeping 6–7 hours is within the recommended adult range and should improve cognitive function, mood, and caregiving safety.
byChoice.zh: 有效：睡眠时间翻倍直接解决了已识别的最急迫生理需求。每晚6–7小时在成人推荐范围内，有助于改善认知功能、情绪和照护安全性。

byChoice.en: Requires further intervention: attending the group is a positive first step, but persistent phone-checking indicates Mrs. Chen has not yet been able to psychologically disengage from the caregiving role during respite time. The nurse should explore her anxiety about being away and discuss building trust incrementally.
byChoice.zh: 需要进一步干预：参加小组是积极的第一步，但全程看手机说明陈女士在喘息时间仍无法在心理上脱离照护角色。护士应探讨她离开时的焦虑并讨论逐步建立信任。

byChoice.en: Requires further intervention: obtaining the telehealth prescription was successful, but failure to follow through with scheduling suggests a persistent barrier — possibly time constraints, guilt about self-care, or logistical issues. The nurse should identify the specific barrier and problem-solve collaboratively.
byChoice.zh: 需要进一步干预：获得远程医疗处方是成功的，但未预约后续治疗提示障碍持续存在——可能是时间限制、对自我照顾的内疚或后勤问题。护士应识别具体障碍并协作解决。

byChoice.en: Effective: a 7-point decrease in the Zarit score in two weeks suggests meaningful improvement in subjective burden. The score remains in the high-burden range (>20), so continued intervention is still needed, but the direction of change indicates the support plan is helping.
byChoice.zh: 有效：两周内Zarit评分下降7分提示主观负担有明显改善。评分仍处于高负担范围（>20分），因此仍需继续干预，但变化方向表明支持计划正在发挥作用。

byChoice.en: Effective: this statement reflects emerging self-care restoration and cognitive reframing — Mrs. Chen still feels guilt (expected in early intervention) but can now acknowledge her husband's safety with the aide and allow herself basic self-care (sitting down to eat). This indicates the reframing intervention and aide training are producing early positive results.
byChoice.zh: 有效：这段话反映了自我照顾的逐步恢复和认知重新框架——陈女士仍感到内疚（干预初期属正常）但已能认可丈夫在护理员照顾下的安全性，并允许自己进行基本的自我照顾（坐下来吃饭）。这表明重新框架干预和护理员培训正在产生早期积极效果。

testTakingStrategy.en: When evaluating outcomes, compare each finding against the baseline data from the initial assessment. Look for measurable changes (e.g., sleep hours, Zarit score) and qualitative shifts in the client's statements. Partial progress still counts as effective if the trend is positive; however, when a prescribed action has not been initiated at all, further intervention is needed regardless of other improvements.
testTakingStrategy.zh: 评价结果时，将每项发现与初始评估的基线数据对比。关注可测量的变化（如睡眠时数、Zarit评分）和患者陈述中的质性转变。如果趋势积极，部分进步仍视为有效；但若处方措施完全未启动，则无论其他方面是否改善都需要进一步干预。

glossary.defZh: 将护理干预后的患者状态与预期目标和基线数据进行比较，以判断干预是否有效。
离开被照护者时产生的过度担忧和不安，常阻碍照护者利用喘息服务。
